[sine]A touch from another world... how long it has been...[/sine]
...Show me the way back home.
One [gold]hammer[/gold] is all I need.
Oh, a guest with a hammer!
Piles of useful [gold]parts[/gold] just for you!
Let me see what you've got.
Rubbish I can always [gold]forge[/gold] into something better.
[thinky_dots]Your hammer... it stirs the [gold]ember[/gold] beneath my scales...[/thinky_dots]
...Those who tend fire deserve respect.
Lend me some of your [gold]heat[/gold].
OROBAS.talk.FRONTIER-SHUMIT_CHARACTER.0-:
Too hard! Too hot!
That hammer... scary! Very scary!!

I won't swing it, calm down.
I only came to ask the way.
VAKUU.talk.FRONTIER-SHUMIT_CHARACTER.0-0:
An otherworldly [sine]smith[/sine], standing before me.
The road home does not lie at the end of that hammer.
VAKUU.talk.FRONTIER-SHUMIT_CHARACTER.0-1:
...Then where does it lie?
Whatever might be an [gold]answer[/gold], I'll take it all.
TEZCATARA.talk.FRONTIER-SHUMIT_CHARACTER:
What a [jitter][b]hot[/b][/jitter] guest! The kiln is always open.
Mind lending some of your [gold]heat[/gold]?

My heat is for hammering, you know.
You wouldn't send me back empty-handed, would you?
Quite the hammer, smith!
But true weapons grow from [gold]beasts[/gold]!
That's your way, not mine.
My work is born on my [gold]anvil[/gold].
NONUPEIPE.talk.FRONTIER-SHUMIT_CHARACTER:
Ah, a visitor with a hammer.
May today's [gold]dice[/gold] favor you.

I wouldn't have brought a hammer if I trusted luck.
Still, I'll roll once.
THE_ARCHITECT.talk.FRONTIER-SHUMIT_CHARA:
So, you climbed all the way up here with a single hammer.
Pioneer of an unfamiliar [gold]world[/gold].

...I don't plan to drag this out.
Just open the [gold]door[/gold] back home, and we'll end this clean.

The door is already beneath your feet.
The only question is whether you are worthy to [b]pay the price[/b].

I've always paid my prices on the [gold]anvil[/gold].
Today is no different.

Very well, smith.
Let a single [jitter]hammer strike[/jitter] settle it.

You again, hammer-bearer.
You could have gone home. Why return?

[gold]Forging[/gold] is never finished in one strike.
You keep hammering until the shape is right.

By now my face must be familiar.
What do you come to hammer today?

Today it's [gold]you[/gold] on the anvil.
Ready?

The [b]smith's song[/b] rings here again.
Come, one last strike.

Last? I'll be the one to decide that.
Now, [gold]begin[/gold].
